mission.title: Install and Configure In Toto on Kubernetes
mission.description: in-toto is a framework to protect supply chain integrity by verifying that each task in the chain is carried out as planned by authorized personnel, ensuring that the product is not tampered with in transit. Installing in-toto allows you to implement robust supply chain security practices in your software development lifecycle.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the in-toto chart.
```shell
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the in-toto Helm chart
steps.description:
Install the in-toto chart using Helm, creating a namespace called 'in-toto' if it doesn't exist.
```shell
helm install in-toto bitnami/chainloop --namespace in-toto --create-namespace --version 4.0.75
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the in-toto pods are running successfully.
```shell
kubectl get pods --namespace in-toto
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the in-toto deployment to optimize resource usage.
```shell
kubectl patch deployment in-toto --namespace in-toto -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"in-toto","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the in-toto Helm release to cleanly remove the application.
```shell
helm uninstall in-toto --namespace in-toto
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any custom resources associated with in-toto to ensure a complete cleanup.
```shell
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep in-toto | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'in-toto' namespace to remove all resources associated with the installation.
```shell
kubectl delete namespace in-toto
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and data.
```shell
helm get values in-toto --namespace in-toto > in-toto-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure that the Helm repository is up to date to access the latest chart versions.
```shell
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Helm release
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the in-toto Helm release to the latest version.
```shell
helm upgrade in-toto bitnami/chainloop --namespace in-toto --version 4.0.75
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the in-toto pods are running successfully after the upgrade.
```shell
kubectl get pods --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the in-toto pods are stuck in Pending state, check for resource availability or scheduling issues.
```shell
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check the error message for details and verify that the chart version is correct.
```shell
helm history in-toto --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the in-toto service is not reachable, check the service configuration and ensure that the correct ports are exposed.
```shell
kubectl get svc --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration changes not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If configuration changes are not applied, ensure that the correct values file was used during the upgrade.
```shell
helm get values in-toto --namespace in-toto
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of in-toto will have all pods running in the 'in-toto' namespace, and you will be able to access the in-toto service. Configuration changes should be reflected in the deployment settings.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have Kubernetes cluster access and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
